entities.name: GROUNDWATER AUGMENTATION
entities.description: The process of increasing groundwater levels, often through rainwater harvesting, to address water security.

entities.name: RAINWATER HARVESTING
entities.description: The collection and storage of rainwater for reuse and groundwater recharge.

entities.name: WATER SECURITY
entities.description: The reliable access to an adequate quantity of acceptable quality water.

entities.name: CLIMATE CHANGE
entities.description: Long-term shifts in temperatures and weather patterns affecting water availability.

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Regions characterized by low rainfall and high evaporation rates.

entities.name: RAINFED AGRICULTURE
entities.description: Agricultural production that relies solely on rainfall for water.

entities.name: GROUNDWATER QUALITY
entities.description: The chemical, physical, and biological characteristics of groundwater.

entities.name: GROUNDWATER TABLE
entities.description: The level below which the ground is saturated with water.

entities.name: MICRO-WATERSHED
entities.description: A small drainage basin area, specifically one spread over 6000 ha in this study.

entities.name: PENINSULAR INDIA
entities.description: The southern part of the Indian subcontinent.

entities.name: RAINFALL
entities.description: Precipitation that falls as rain, influencing groundwater levels and quality.

entities.name: RAINWATER HARVESTING STRUCTURES
entities.description: Physical installations used to capture and store rainwater for augmentation.

entities.name: GROUNDWATER WITHDRAWALS
entities.description: The removal of water from an aquifer for various uses.

entities.name: PRINCIPAL COMPONENT ANALYSIS
entities.description: A statistical procedure used to analyze spatial and temporal variations in groundwater quality.

entities.name: HIERARCHAL CLUSTERING
entities.description: A method of cluster analysis used to analyze groundwater quality variations.

entities.name: HYDROGEOCHEMICAL PROCESSES
entities.description: Chemical reactions and processes that govern groundwater quality.

entities.name: PREFERENTIAL FLOW RECHARGE PROCESSES
entities.description: The rapid movement of water through macropores or fractures, affecting groundwater quality.

entities.name: FERTILIZER USE
entities.description: The application of nutrients to croplands, acting as a non-point source of nitrogen.

entities.name: CROPLANDS
entities.description: Land used for growing crops where fertilizers are applied.

entities.name: NO3–N
entities.description: Nitrate-nitrogen, a chemical component in groundwater often derived from fertilizers.

entities.name: NH4–N
entities.description: Ammonium-nitrogen, a chemical component in groundwater often derived from fertilizers.

entities.name: BURIAL SITE
entities.description: A point source of groundwater contamination.

entities.name: GROUNDWATER CONTAMINATION
entities.description: The presence of pollutants in groundwater, such as from burial sites.

entities.name: DRINKING WATER
entities.description: Water intended for human consumption, subject to quality guidelines.

entities.name: FLUORIDE
entities.description: A chemical element in groundwater that can be a point of concern for drinking water.

entities.name: SALINITY
entities.description: The dissolved salt content of water, which can cause salt build-up in soil.

entities.name: SODIUM HAZARD
entities.description: The risk associated with high sodium levels in water affecting soil and crops.

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The medium in which crops grow, susceptible to salt build-up.

entities.name: CROP WATER AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The amount of water available for crop uptake, affected by soil salinity.

entities.name: TELANGANA STATE
entities.description: A state in India where the study was conducted.

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the study was conducted.
